data.change_summary: Fixed the remaining `BeadStarted` journal dedupe collision. Start events now serialize structured details with `project_id`, `run_id`, and `plan_hash`, `record_bead_start` writes those canonical details from the task-run entry, and the filesystem journal adapter can repair same-attempt start replays in place instead of appending duplicates. Added regressions for same-attempt runless-to-named backfill and for distinct named runs that share a `started_at` but must keep separate start journal rows.
outstanding_risks: Milestone mutations still span separate lineage, snapshot, and journal files without a cross-file transaction, so a crash between file writes can still leave snapshot or journal state temporarily behind `task-runs.ndjson` until the same operation is replayed or state is reconciled from lineage.

Manually corrupted lineage that already contains multiple simultaneous open attempts for the same bead still rejects new starts or runless completions as ambiguous until the bad state is repaired or richer caller metadata is supplied.

steps.description: Added shared start-journal detail rendering in `model.rs` and switched `record_bead_start` to persist structured start-event metadata derived from the canonical task-run entry.

Extended the filesystem journal adapter with `BeadStarted` repair/merge logic so same-attempt start replays backfill missing `run_id` or `plan_hash` without collapsing distinct named runs that happen to share the same timestamp.
Updated milestone_record regressions to cover runless-to-named start replay repair and same-timestamp distinct named start events, then revalidated formatting, milestone_record tests, and clippy.
